prompts: A microservices architecture is composed of small, independently deployable services, each with its own data and dedicated functionality, enabling scalability and flexibility. It enhances scalability by allowing independent scaling of services according to demand, ensuring efficient resource utilization. Inter-service communication typically occurs via lightweight protocols like HTTP/REST or messaging systems such as Kafka. Decoupling is achieved through bounded contexts wherein each microservice is responsible for a specific business capability, reducing interdependencies and enabling independent updates and deployments, fostering agile development and continuous delivery. This architecture supports robust, resilient systems adaptable to changing business needs.
